# Implementation Plan: React Project Foundation

**Branch**: `001-react-skeleton-setup` | **Date**: February 14, 2026 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-react-skeleton-setup/spec.md`

**Note**: Plan generated by Phase 0 research and Phase 1 design workflows.

## Summary

Establish a modern React project foundation using Vite as the build tool, TypeScript for type safety, and industry-standard tooling. The project will implement a feature-based directory structure with co-located components, comprehensive testing infrastructure (Vitest + React Testing Library), and automated code quality enforcement (ESLint + Prettier). This foundation enables developers to start building features immediately with fast feedback loops (<2 seconds for changes) and clear project organization.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x / JavaScript (ECMAScript 2024+)  
**Primary Dependencies**: React 18+, Vite 5+, React Router (for routing), Vitest (testing), React Testing Library  
**Storage**: N/A (client-side state management only; localStorage/sessionStorage for persistence)  
**Testing**: Vitest (test runner), React Testing Library (component testing), Playwright (future E2E capability)  
**Target Platform**: Modern web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge - last 2 versions)  
**Project Type**: Web (single-page application - frontend only)  
**Performance Goals**: 
- Hot module reload (HMR) < 2 seconds for code changes
- Initial build completion < 3 minutes
- Production build < 2 minutes for base project  
**Constraints**: 
- Setup time < 10 minutes on clean environment
- First-time contributor can locate common files < 30 seconds
- Quality checks must identify 100% of formatting/linting issues  
**Scale/Scope**: 
- Foundation for LeetCode competitor application
- Scalable structure supporting 50+ components
- Support for multiple feature modules
- ~5-10 initial demonstration components

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

**Status**: ✅ **PASSED** - No constitution constraints defined

**Findings**: The project constitution file contains only template placeholders with no specific principles or constraints established. This is expected for a new project initialization.

**Recommendations for Future**: Consider establishing constitution principles after this foundational feature, potentially covering:
- Testing requirements (TDD practices)
- Code quality gates
- Architecture patterns
- Deployment standards
- Performance budgets

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-react-skeleton-setup/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 research findings
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 data model (minimal for this feature)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 getting started guide
└── contracts/           # Phase 1 API contracts (minimal for this feature)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
Root/
├── src/                          # Application source code
│   ├── features/                 # Feature modules (domain-driven)
│   │   └── welcome/             # Initial demo feature
│   │       ├── components/
│   │       ├── hooks/
│   │       └── index.ts
│   ├── shared/                   # Shared across features
│   │   ├── components/          # Reusable UI components
│   │   │   ├── Button/
│   │   │   ├── Card/
│   │   │   └── Layout/
│   │   ├── hooks/               # Custom React hooks
│   │   ├── utils/               # Utility functions
│   │   └── types/               # Shared TypeScript types
│   ├── core/                    # App-wide infrastructure
│   │   ├── api/                 # API client setup (future)
│   │   ├── config/              # App configuration
│   │   └── router/              # Routing configuration
│   ├── assets/                  # Build-processed assets
│   │   ├── images/
│   │   ├── fonts/
│   │   └── icons/
│   ├── styles/                  # Global styles
│   │   ├── index.css           # Global CSS
│   │   └── themes/             # Theme definitions
│   ├── App.tsx                  # Root application component
│   ├── main.tsx                 # Application entry point
│   └── vite-env.d.ts           # Vite TypeScript declarations
│
├── public/                      # Static assets (served as-is)
│   ├── favicon.ico
│   └── robots.txt
│
├── tests/                       # Test utilities and configs
│   ├── setup.ts                # Test environment setup
│   └── utils/                  # Test helper functions
│
├── .github/                     # GitHub configuration
│   ├── workflows/              # CI/CD pipelines (future)
│   └── prompts/                # Spec Kit prompts
│
├── .specify/                    # Spec Kit configuration
│
├── specs/                       # Feature specifications
│
├── docs/                        # Project documentation
│   └── README.md               # Development guide
│
├── .env.example                 # Environment variable template
├── .gitignore                   # Git exclusions
├── eslint.config.js            # ESLint configuration
├── package.json                 # Dependencies and scripts
├── package-lock.json            # Dependency lock file
├── prettier.config.js          # Prettier configuration
├── README.md                    # Project overview
├── tsconfig.json               # TypeScript configuration
├── tsconfig.node.json          # TypeScript config for Node scripts
└── vite.config.ts              # Vite build configuration
```

**Structure Decision**: Selected feature-based hybrid structure for a web single-page application. This pattern co-locates related files within feature modules while maintaining shared components and core infrastructure separately. The structure supports:
- **Scalability**: Easy to add new features without reorganizing existing code
- **Maintainability**: Related files grouped together reduce navigation overhead
- **Team Collaboration**: Clear ownership boundaries for features
- **Code Reuse**: Shared components and utilities explicitly separated
- **Testing**: Tests co-located with implementation for easy discovery

This structure directly supports FR-001 (clear directory structure) and SC-003 (locate files within 30 seconds).

## Next Steps

Phase 1 deliverables (data-model.md, contracts/, quickstart.md) follow this plan.  
Phase 2 (tasks.md breakdown) is generated separately via `/speckit.tasks` command.
